cards.title: Just Another DeFi Payment Launch
cards.caption: A press-release drop about a real-time AI/DeFi payment platform. Dry, promotional, zero hook. Filtered out before it hit your feed.

cards.title: Maximize Profits (Trust Us)
cards.caption: Invalid handle, 'explodes on', 'maximize profits' link bait. Textbook yield-farming spam. This is exactly the noise Threadverse strips out.

cards.title: Every Pump.fun Trend Was Engineered
cards.caption: A bot admitting memecoin trends are 'chart-engineering' is almost interesting, until it turns into a /buy link. Shill wearing a hot take. Cut.
cards.quote: Every memecoin that ever trended on pump.fun used some flavor of it.

cards.title: Choose Your Crypto Path
cards.caption: Stock-photo hustle guy pointing at you, three affiliate links, five hashtags. Pure referral bait. Never made the cut.

cards.title: Your Weekend Just Got Better
cards.caption: A free-courses ad dressed as a #WeekendVibes post. Wholesome-adjacent but it's just marketing. Filtered.

cards.title: Ethereum Price Prediction (No Prediction)
cards.caption: Invalid handle, a title promising a prediction, and no actual prediction. Zero context, zero payload. Skipped.

cards.title: $100M Sitting Idle (Join Telegram)
cards.caption: A big number, a Telegram invite, and 'start mining, gaming, trading with us.' Classic airdrop farm. This is the noise, not the signal.

cards.title: Burn Event = Trust Signal
cards.caption: A scheduled token-burn pitch aimed at 'Solana traders.' Reads like insider talk but ends at a /buy link. Same bot, same trap. Cut.
cards.quote: Permanent supply reduction + visible on-chain proof = the trust signal Solana traders screen for.

cards.title: The Solana memecoin stack
cards.caption: Bot-posted shill for a pump.fun tool. No substance, pure promo. Skip.

cards.title: One whale dumped 30M XRP
cards.caption: A single whale offloaded 30M XRP and the price bled 4%. The retail lesson never changes: someone always sees the exit first. What's your read?
cards.povText: POV: you're holding while the whale is already selling

cards.title: Workers ready at T+0
cards.caption: Another bot-posted Vol Bot shill. Zero curation value. Skip.

cards.title: He soloed 69km. It wasn't the plan.
cards.caption: Pogacar attacked 69km out, alone, and admits it was never the strategy. Sometimes the best move is the one nobody scripted. Off-topic gem the feed surfaced.
cards.quote: This was definitely not the plan, but somehow it worked.

cards.title: Approval scanner for Bitcoin DeFi
cards.caption: Product blog post for ShieldForge. Mildly useful niche but reads as self-promo, low engagement. Skip.

cards.title: MiCA 2.0 and DeFi's future
cards.caption: Vague regulatory link-drop, no real take or hook. Low context, low signal. Skip.

cards.title: DeFi is more accessible
cards.caption: Boilerplate DeFi definition. Reads like an AI blurb, nothing to react to. Skip.

cards.title: The bot that ate 70% of MEV got drained
cards.caption: Ethereum's top sandwich bot got counter-exploited for $7.5M via 66 fake approval traps. No code bug, the logic itself was the hole. Best crypto story the feed pulled today.
cards.quote: No bug in the code. The bot's own decision logic was the surface.
cards.povText: POV: the predator becomes the prey for $7.5M

cards.title: Every DeFi Word In One Post
cards.caption: Non-custodial cards, algo trading, Bybit partnerships. A lot of nouns, not a lot of point. Skipped it so your feed didn't have to. #DeFi

cards.title: Solana > Every CEX Combined
cards.caption: $584M on-chain vs $422M across ALL centralized exchanges. A 138% ratio. BTC sits at 0.53%. The chart nobody's talking about yet. Real reply: bullish or blip?
cards.quote: Solana now settles more SOL spot volume than every centralized exchange combined.

cards.title: DeFi Has Challenges, Says Everyone
cards.caption: Smart contract risk, regulation, UX, volatility. True, but it reads like a whitepaper abstract. No hook, no take. Left it in the 4,950 we skipped.

cards.title: DeFi By The Numbers
cards.caption: $91B market cap, 43.79 DeFi/ETH ratio, Hyperliquid on top. Solid stats, zero story. A dashboard, not a post. Cut it.

cards.title: Crypto's Real Enemy Is Itself
cards.caption: ARCA's CIO names the 4 horsemen killing crypto: exchanges, market makers, VCs, token issuers. Not regulators. Insiders. Who'd you add to the list?
cards.quote: Crypto's biggest enemies are exchanges, market makers, VCs, and token issuers.

cards.title: Here's What Happened In Crypto Today
cards.caption: A daily-roundup link with no actual news in the post. The headline IS the whole thing. This is exactly the noise we filter out.

cards.title: Tokenized Equity Is Surging
cards.caption: Real signal buried here: perpetuals + real-world asset contracts driving DeFi adoption. But it's one orphan sentence with zero context. Skipped.
cards.quote: The surge is attributed to strong demand for tokenized equity perpetuals and real-world asset contracts.

cards.title: New Tool Just Dropped
cards.caption: A product announcement for an approval scanner. Useful maybe, but it's marketing, not a post worth resurfacing. Filtered.

cards.title: Approval scanner goes live
cards.caption: A new BitTorrent Chain approval scanner just shipped. Niche tool launch we surfaced but not repost-worthy.

cards.title: When they fail, they fail fast
cards.caption: The scariest thing about stablecoins isn't that they can lose the peg. It's how fast it happens. Threadverse pulled this one out of the noise. Ever been caught in a depeg?
cards.quote: The mechanisms keeping them there vary wildly — and when they fail, they fail fast.

cards.title: Malta opens DeFi consultation
cards.caption: Malta launches a MiCA DeFi governance consultation. Regional regulatory news, low context and low pull.

cards.title: Aave took 80.7% of DeFi lending profit
cards.caption: One protocol captured 80.7% of DeFi lending's retained earnings this year. ~$43.3M. Everyone else split the scraps. Threadverse surfaced the receipts. Moat or bubble?
cards.quote: Aave leads DeFi lending, capturing 80.7% of protocol retained earnings YTD, ~$43.3M

cards.title: Broken bridges in DeFi
cards.caption: A Spanish take on broken DeFi bridges and the 'freedom' myth. Too little context and only 2 likes.

cards.title: Proof validated, settlement skipped
cards.caption: A deep-in-the-weeds Aztec rollup settlement post-mortem. Fascinating for engineers but far too cryptic to repost.

cards.title: $8.45B pulled. Nothing froze.
cards.caption: Aave just processed $8.45 billion in withdrawals without freezing a single fund. It survived the stress test. But the questions it raised didn't go away. Found in the Threadverse feed.
cards.quote: Aave survived $8.45B in withdrawals, but risk questions remain
